quotes.verbatim_quote: these rhythms are twice as long as the ones in Chapter 20.
In this chapter we will consider five different time zones or gears to play in.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Taxonomy of five rhythmic subdivisions as interchangeable gears.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
The aim of this chapter is to expand your vocabulary by adapting lines to these varying time zones
or gears.

To make this line feel comfortable
in triplet gear you need to leave off the first four or the last four notes. In this case we'll leave off
the last four.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Mechanical rule for fitting eight-note phrase into triplet subdivision.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Set your metronome between 50 and 60 for the half note and play the line in its origi-
nal form. While playing the line as eighth notes take notice of your articulations and accents.

Continue repeating the line until you are in the groove of triplets and phrasing them as if they
were eighth notes.
quotes.why_load_bearing: Phrasing triplets with eighth-note articulation logic.

quotes.verbatim_quote:
Next try playing the line as quarter-note triplets. Again repeat the line over and over until you feel
that groove.
